summary_one_line: A SELECT keyword prefix followed by a long valid expression body that drives the Lemon parser into this yy_reduce reduce action.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must begin with the literal SQL keyword 'SELECT' (bytes 53 45 4c 45 43 54) and continue with a long, well-formed expression body (runs of '+', '~', digits, ':' and ';') so the SQLite Lemon parser performs the deep reduce step at yy_reduce:157670 (true branch).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds open with the 'SELECT' keyword and a long expression tail; the parser drives yy_reduce far enough that the blocker's true side fires (T=9) and the enclosing reduce path is exercised hundreds of times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every mopt loser-blocking seed is short and starts with non-keyword bytes (0d/5c/3b/8b/bb), never the 'SELECT' prefix, so the parser bails early, never reaches the reduce action, and the blocker true side stays at T=0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The technique delta is mopt mutation: both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token operator set, but mopt's MOpt particle-swarm operator scheduling failed to assemble/preserve the 'SELECT'+long-expression structure that naive's uniform random selection plus TokenInsert/TokenReplace reached. naive (baseline scheduling) wins; the divergence is attributable to MOpt's scheduled operator distribution being worse for this structured-SQL goal.

evidence_trail.claim: The single decisive pair is naive beating mopt with the technique delta mopt_mutation.
The blocker is in the Lemon parser reduce action yy_reduce and its true side is globally blocked.
The blocker is reached only after the parser call chain runs the parser on input.
Winner seeds begin with the literal SELECT keyword bytes.
Loser seeds are short and start with non-keyword bytes, not SELECT.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 0d 64 64 04 00 ac ac ac ac ac ac                  .dd........

evidence_trail.claim: At offsets 0x00-0x05 the winner bytes are concentrated on the SELECT keyword while the loser bytes are scattered non-keyword values.
The blocker true side fires for the winner role and never for the loser role.
mopt shares naive's operator set but selects/stacks operators via MOpt particle-swarm scheduling instead of uniform random selection.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the same havoc + token operator set as naive, but driven
by `StdMOptMutator`

evidence_trail.claim: naive builds keyword structure via uniform havoc plus TokenInsert/TokenReplace dictionary operators.
A winner seed was discovered using TokenReplace, evidencing dictionary-driven keyword construction under naive scheduling.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is mandatory only for the I2SRandReplace claim; this branch's technique delta is mopt_mutation (naive winner over mopt loser), so no I2S floor check applies. Mechanism is read directly from the MECHANISM CONTEXT operator-scheduling difference and the SELECT-prefix byte signal.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic SELECT-prefixed deep-expression harness in which mopt resolves the reduce branch as fast as or faster than naive across trials, showing MOpt scheduling does not disadvantage assembling this structured-SQL input.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that MOpt's scheduling specifically degrades assembly of the SELECT-keyword structure (vs naive's uniform selection) is inferred from operator-set parity plus seed structure, not from a direct per-operator success-rate comparison.
